snap_subtitle: Ultralight wireless FPS mouse that finally fits small hands.
keytakeaway: The Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2c is a compact wireless gaming mouse built for competitive FPS players, especially those using fingertip or aggressive claw grips in CS2 and Valorant. Its 51 g solid-shell body, shorter 118.4 mm length, and HERO 2 + LIGHTSPEED platform deliver effortless lift-reset control with reliable tracking and stable wireless performance. Optional 8K polling is available for high-refresh rigs, while 1K–2K keeps battery life strong for daily ranked queues.
pcard: The Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2c is a **compact ultralight wireless gaming mouse** tuned for FPS play, pairing a **51 g** solid shell with the full **HERO 2 + LIGHTSPEED + LIGHTFORCE** stack. If the full-size Superlight felt a bit long, this shorter shell frees fingertip/claw control without sacrificing reliability. Prefer the classic length or a right-hand ergo? Compare <pop=mouse-94>Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2</pop> and <pop=mouse-91>Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 Dex</pop>.
verdict:
The Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2c is a compact **wireless gaming mouse** built for FPS players who value nimble lift-and-reset control in **CS2**, **Valorant**, and similarly twitchy shooters. By scaling the familiar Superlight geometry into a shorter 118.4 mm shell at just 51 g (solid, no honeycomb), it keeps the “Series 2” platform intact—**HERO 2** sensor, **LIGHTSPEED** wireless, **LIGHTFORCE** hybrid switches, onboard memory, and **USB-C**—so you get elite tracking without turning to a niche “mini” with compromises.

#### Lineup context
Within Logitech’s current trio, the 2c is the **agility specialist**. If you want the same platform in a longer, steadier shell, see the <pop=mouse-94>Pro X Superlight 2</pop>; for a right-hand ergonomic take, there’s the <pop=mouse-91>Pro X Superlight 2 Dex</pop>.

#### What you feel in game
Small-to-medium hands and fingertip/aggressive-claw grips benefit most. The shorter body avoids the tail pressing into your palm, opening up vertical micro-adjustments and quick re-centers on low sens. That translates to cleaner recoil control and target-to-target transitions. Despite the featherweight, the body feels rigid and “sealed,” so it’s fast in hand but not flimsy.

> A 51 g **solid-shell** design plus stable wireless makes the 2c feel quick and dependable—not delicate.

#### Performance spine
HERO 2’s headroom (100–44,000 DPI, up to 888 IPS / 88G) effectively removes spinout risk on modern pads, while LIGHTSPEED maintains a consistent link you can stop thinking about. The mouse supports polling **up to 8,000 Hz** for high-refresh setups; many players will still prefer 1K–2K or 4K to balance smoothness, battery life, and CPU load. Battery is rated around **95 hours** at standard settings, unusually strong for this weight class.

| Spec | What you’ll notice |
|---|---|
| 51 g, solid shell | Low inertia for faster starts/stops; easy lift-reset without flex. |
| 118.4 × 61.2 × 38.6 mm | Less palm contact; more fingertip/claw freedom. |
| Up to 8K polling | Finer motion updates on 360–540 Hz displays (with battery/CPU trade-offs). |

#### Clicks, side buttons, and coating
LIGHTFORCE main clicks are crisp and defined, but a bit **firmer and louder** than ultra-light mechanicals—great for preventing accidental presses during aggressive repositioning, less ideal if you spam-click. The **side buttons** remain the family weak spot: they work reliably but can feel **mushy** with extra travel compared to top competitors. Coating is a functional matte; the included pre-cut grip tape is a nice “tune it your way” option.

#### Bottom line
If the full-size Superlight has always felt just a bit too long, the **Superlight 2c** is the rare compact that keeps flagship hardware, battery efficiency, and wireless stability intact. It’s a top-tier fit for small-to-medium hands in fingertip/claw, with trade-offs mainly in side-button tactility and price. If you want a flatter, even more fingertip-leaning premium compact, consider the <pop=mouse-127>Razer Viper Mini Signature Edition</pop>; for a narrow claw specialist feel, look at the <pop=mouse-193>Endgame Gear OP1w 4k Wireless</pop>. For broader shopping, see our [more ultra-light wireless mice](/hubs/mouse?connection=hybrid&weight=36-60).

#### Key takeaways
- Compact **Superlight geometry** that finally suits smaller hands and fingertip/aggressive claw.
- **HERO 2 + LIGHTSPEED** delivers “set and forget” tracking and wireless.
- Optional **8K polling** helps on very high refresh rigs; 1K–4K is the practical sweet spot.
- Downsides: side buttons feel average, clicks run firm/loud, and pricing sits on the premium side.
pros: Shorter 118.4 mm; better fingertip/claw* HERO 2 tracking; spinout-proof
Up to 8K polling (optional)*USB-C charging; grip tape included
cons: No Bluetooth; high polling drains battery
Compact shape poor for large palm grips
